cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the two statements is sarcastic. The options are A and B. Let me read them again carefully. Option A says: "One of the worst things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." Option B says: "One of the enjoyable things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." So, the question is asking which one is sarcastic. Sarcasm usually involves saying the opposite of what is meant, often with a tone of mock praise or irony. Let me break it down. The task mentioned is "pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." In retail, handling returns can be a tedious
